title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

This choropleth world map displays the status of countries regarding Gavi support for the year 2024. The legend distinguishes between two categories: countries eligible for support, shown in teal, and countries not eligible, shown in light beige. The teal shading indicates nations that meet the criteria of having a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810 over the past three years. A large, contiguous block of countries across Sub-Saharan Africa is colored teal, extending from the western coast through the central region and down to the southern tip, including nations such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Ethiopia. In Asia, the teal band covers a significant portion of the continent, including India, Pakistan, Afghanistan, and parts of Southeast Asia like Myanmar and Cambodia. Several countries in the Middle East, such as Yemen and Syria, also appear in teal, alongside North Korea in East Asia. In contrast, North America, South America, Europe, Australia, and Russia are predominantly colored in the light beige shade, indicating they are not eligible for this specific support. The map visually separates the globe into these two distinct groups based on the income threshold defined in the subtitle, with the eligible nations concentrated primarily in Africa and parts of Asia.
